title: Lupinaster lemmonii
intro: Lupinus lemmonii, commonly known as Lemmon's lupine, is a perennial herbaceous plant that belongs to the legume family (Fabaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the management of various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Lemmon's lupine include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Lupinus lemmonii are teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Lupinaster lemmonii is a species of flowering plant that belongs to the family Oleaceae. It is a member of the genus Lupinaster, which is part of the order Lamiales. Within the Oleaceae family, Lupinaster lemmonii is classified as a shrub or small tree. The classification of Lupinaster lemmonii is also related to its botanical name, which indicates its botanical classification. Further classification can be broken down to include its kingdom, phylum, and class, all of which are Plantae.
